Shield of Shielding is a rare Shield that allows the wearer to cast Shield and use Shield Bash.

Named in the style of Extremely Unimaginative Jimmy, the geographer and interior decorator who named the 'waterfall' and the 'fireplace'.

name: Shortsword +2
desc:
Shortsword +2 is a rare, moderately enchanted (+2) variant of the Shortswords family of weapons. It is a martial melee weapon wielded in one hand. As a finesse weapon, it can benefit from the wielder's Dexterity and not just their Strength. It's a light weapon that anyone can dual-wield without special training.

A common and effective sword, carried by adventurers, thieves, and watchmen alike.

name: Spear +2
desc:
Spear +2 is a rare, moderately enchanted (+2) Spear.

Even the ornamentation at the base of this spear's head is honed to a razor edge.

name: Shield +2
desc:
A Shield +2 is a rare Shield with an enchantment increasing its  Armour Class bonus.

This robust shield has been polished to a bright sheen.

name: The Sparkle Hands
desc:
The Sparkle Hands are a pair of rare Gloves that grant Lightning Charges  on unarmed attacks and provide Advantage against metal constructs and armoured foes while charged.

'Sometimes I just want to feel that spark,' Yrre the Sparkstruck said.

name: The Watersparkers
desc:
The Watersparkers are a pair of rare Boots that allow the wearer to electrify water surfaces and gain Lightning Charges.

An enormous storm was raging the first time Yrre the Sparkstruck left the Underdark. The deep gnome's interest in lightning became at once an all-consuming obsession, and they would spend the rest of their years striving to recreate that storm's power themselves.

name: The Speedy Lightfeet
desc:
The Speedy Lightfeet are a pair of rare Boots that allow the wearer to gain Lightning Charges when Dashing in combat.

It is said the Speedy Lightfeet were the first of many lightning inventions made by Yrre the Sparkstruck. The boots' old, frayed edges zap and crackle with even the slightest movement.

name: The Spellsparkler
desc:
The Spellsparkler is a rare Quarterstaff that allows the wielder to generate Lightning Charges upon dealing damage with spells.

Yrre the Sparkstruck intended to give the Spellsparkler to Lenore, a cleric of Mystra and old lover. But when the gnome returned to the cleric's tower, they found it empty - inhabited only by dust and abandoned experiments.

name: Uncovered Mysteries
desc:
Uncovered Mysteries is a rare Amulet that allows the wearer to cast Detect Thoughts once per Long Rest.

Beneath the layers of dust covering this necklace, its inlaid gems still give off a blue sparkle - the colour of Mystra's clergy.

name: The Sparky Points
desc: The Sparky Points is a rare Trident that allows the wielder to generate Lightning Charges upon dealing melee damage with this weapon.

name: The Protecty Sparkswall
desc:
The Protecty Sparkswall is a rare Clothing that increases the wearer's defences while they have Lightning Charges and grants a +1 bonus to their Spell Save DC.

Yrre the Sparkstruck was raised in the Underdark, so they weren't surprised that power begets envy, and envy begets violence. They were prepared.

name: Titanstring Bow
desc:
Titanstring Bow is a rare +1 Longbow that allows its wielder to add their Strength Modifier to damage dealt with this weapon.

The commonly held belief that archers are rangey, thin-wristed people is completely false. It takes years of training to pull a bowstring all the way back to your cheekbone, and wrists like iron.

name: True Love's Caress
desc:
True Love's Caress is one of a matching pair of Rings that grants the wearer  Warding Bond.

The most pragmatic and courageous lover ever known was, of course, Sorcha MacTire, for whom the popular hairstyle is named, and for whom many an extremely soppy poem has been written (most of them comparing her to geography).

name: Thermodynamo Axe
desc:
Thermodynamo Axe is a rare, lightly enchanted (+1) Battleaxe that grants Heat whenever the wielder deals damage with this weapon.

Designed by gnomish innovation, this weapon has a dynamic core that swivels and tinkles into life whenever the axe is swung at a foe.

name: Thorn Blade
desc:
Thorn Blade is a rare, lightly enchanted (+1) Scimitar that deals 1d4⁠⁠Poison damage with melee weapons attacks while Concentrating and grants the ability to cast  Ensnaring Strike (Melee).

This Blade is sharp and slender as a serpent's fang - and just as likely to inflict a hideous sting.

name: True Love's Embrace
desc:
True Love's Embrace is one of a matching pair of Rings that lets the wearer cast  Warding Bond.

The scholar of love, Joel Firris Baldidims, writes that the next time he reads about a woman compared to a flower (or any feature of geography for that matter) he's going to start knocking on poets' doors with a sledgehammer.

name: Twist of Fortune
desc:
Twist of Fortune is a rare +1 Morningstar with the unique weapon action,  Blood Money. When the wielder rolls 2 or less with this weapon's damage die, they reroll and take the new result.

They say rich folk are never truly happy; especially true when they're targeted with this morningstar.

name: Thunderpalm Strikers
desc:
Thunderpalm Strikers are a rare pair of Gloves that add Thunder damage to unarmed attacks and allow the wearer to cast Thunderwave once per Long Rest.

Each blow landed while wearing these gloves produces the rumbling boom of a thousand demons' ravenous stomachs.

name: Till Death Do Us Part
desc:
Til Death Do Us Part is a rare Ring that grants its wearer the ability to cast  Beacon of Hope.

Love is the sweetest bird that might migrate to the charming country of your heart.

name: The Pointy Hat
desc:
The Pointy Hat is a rare hat that gives the wearer a bonus to their persuasion checks equal to their Intelligence modifier.

Sages over the course of ages have dealt very constructive and useful advice - however, none so sagacious as that of the wizard Otiluke, who once advised one of his students thusly: 'Never cast Speak with Dead on a loved one if there's unresolved tension there. It's never worth it.

name: Tightening Orbit Helm
desc: Tightening Orbit Helm is a rare Helmet.

name: Treacleflow Amulet
desc:
Treacleflow Amulet is a rare Amulet.

The air surrounding this lavishly decorated amulet is thick - like pushing your fingers through sap or syrup.

name: Torment Drinker Armour
desc:
Torment Drinker Armour is a rare Light Armour that grants temporary hit points when the wearer scores a Critical Hit or kills a creature.

Timid, god-fearing Cat Burroughs was dizzyingly happy when an angel courted her one summer. But in the autumn, when he shamefacedly revealed he was not an angel but an incubus of the Hells, Cat flew into a rage, slew him, and fashioned this armour from his wings and still-warm bones.

name: Trident +2
desc:
Trident +2 is a rare, moderately enchanted (+2) variant of the Tridents family of weapons. It is a martial melee weapon that can be wielded in one hand, or with both hands for extra damage.    Its design lends itself well to be thrown at enemies as a projectile.

An elegant, vicious weapon designed to eviscerate flesh and organs.

name: Adamantine Splint Armour
desc:
Adamantine Splint Armour is a very rare Heavy Armour that reduces all incoming damage by 2 and sends attackers Reeling. In addition, attacks can't land critical hits on the wearer.

The adamantine plates lock and slide together perfectly - offering protection against even the deadliest of blades.

name: Adamantine Scale Mail
desc:
Adamantine Scale Mail is a very rare Medium Armour that reduces all incoming damage by 1 and sends attackers Reeling. In addition, attacks cannot land Critical Hits on the user.

The adamantine scales hug your body like a second skin - a heavy, near-impregnable skin.

name: Armour of Agility
desc:
The Armour of Agility is a very rare Medium Armour that doesn't limit your AC's Dexterity bonus, and boosts all of the user's Saving Throws by 2.

Layered sheets of smelted, shaped, and smithed steel have been etched with a sweetly luminous gold. Its outward appearance is heavy. And yet it's so light when you lift it, like lifting a bag of shrunken dead leaves.

name: Armour of Persistence
desc:
Armour of Persistence is a very rare Armour that grants its wearer permanent Resistance and Blade Ward effects in addition to reducing all damage taken by 2.

Great sheets of steel were hammered and bashed into something like a carapace, thick and dense - you can almost feel the hammer blows, flooded with rich magic.

name: Abyss Beckoners
desc:
The Abyss Beckoners are a very rare pair of gloves. They improve the damage resistance of the wearer's summons at the risk of driving them Mad.

Demons will bite their masters, slaves, comrades, and, on occasion, themselves. They bite on principle, and are functionally piranha with extra steps.

name: Amulet of Greater Health
desc:
Amulet of Greater Health is a very rare Amulet that can be obtained from the House of Hope.

Worn by Lumi, a cleric beholden to twilight, who sheltered her fellow adventurers as a bantam shelters her chicks.

name: Arcane Absorption Dagger
desc:
Arcane Absorption Dagger is a very rare, lightly enchanted (+1) variant of the Daggers family of weapons. It is a simple melee weapon wielded in one hand. As a finesse weapon, it can benefit from the wielder's Dexterity and not just their Strength. It's a light weapon that anyone can dual-wield without special training.  Its design lends itself well to be thrown at enemies as a projectile.

The leather grip of this knife is decorated with jugs.

name: Belm
desc:
Belm is a very rare +2 Scimitar. It grants the Whirlwind Attack and Perfectly Balanced Strike weapon actions.

Faded but still legible, the mark of a  Kara-Turan forgemaster adorns the pommel of this exquisite sword. Light as a robin's wing, quick as a gnomish wit, this weapon has unseamed many foes - and will continue to, in the correct hands.

name: Arrow of Dragon Slaying
desc:
Arrow of Dragon Slaying is a Consumable (Arrow) item that can be fired from a Ranged Weapon.

It's said the inventor of these arrows was assassinated by a shadow dragon, who wished their secret for itself.

name: Armour of Landfall
desc:
Armour of Landfall is a very rare light armour which improves the spellcasting ability of its wearer and grants  Advantage on Constitution  Saving Throws. The wearer also heals while standing in vines and can cast  Plant Growth.

Smelling of long-abandoned gardens grown wild with thorny roses, moss and hibiscus, this armour was worn by a powerful druid who spent so long from people that she forgot to miss them.

name: Armour of the Sporekeeper
desc:
Armour of the Sporekeeper is a very rare Light Armour that enhances the wearer's Necrotic damage and Symbiotic Entity ability with 3 new abilities.

According to one story, this armour was enriched by the cascading spores of a myconid sovereign, its great fungal head shimmering and twinkling as it shook upon the material.

name: Armour of Moonbasking
desc:
Armour of Moonbasking is a very rare Light Armour that bestows Temporary Hit Points and Armour Class to its wielder when they Wild Shape.

This armour was designed by lunar-obsessed Cuthbertina, a gnomish arcane smith who now lives peacefully on a mountain plateau with her magical forge.

name: Amulet of Windrider
desc:
Amulet of Windrider is a very rare Amulet that allows the wearer to cast Ride the Winds and Gust of Wind. It is one of the rewards of the quest Save Vanra.

Heavily embossed with filigree, this necklace reflects light in all directions.

name: Abdel's Trusted Shield
desc:
Abdel's Trusted Shield is a very rare Shield that allows the wearer to cast Protection from Missiles once per Short Rest, as well as use Shield Bash.

Attached to this shield is a faded note. 'G, I've found the best defence against an arrow is to be the one behind the bow - but this shield also does the trick in a pinch.

name: Amulet of the Devout
desc:
The Amulet of the Devout is a very rare Amulet. It grants the wearer a +2 bonus to Spell Save DC and, if the wearer has access to it, an additional use of  Channel Divinity Charge.

Otto, a member of the powerful arcane organisation the Circle of Eight, had a penchant for amulets. So much so that they announced his arrival before he ever needed to.

name: Bhaalist Gloves
desc:
Bhaalist Gloves are a very rare pair of Gloves that allow the wearer to Garrotte a target.

When the Lord of Murder, Bhaal, was rendered mortal, he knew his days were numbered. His vile machinations had gained him many enemies. Those days ran out and Bhaal was indeed killed, not by a god, but by a man with hawkish features named  Cyric.

name: Bhaalist Armour
desc:
Bhaalist Armour is a very rare Light Armour which grants Aura of Murder and a bonus to Initiative rolls.

Bhaal, Lord of Murder, was forced to walk the world as a mortal man during a period of history called the  Time of Troubles. He was more vulnerable than he had ever been. But Bhaal was cunning. He had contingencies in case he died.

name: Assassin of Bhaal Cowl
desc:
Assassin of Bhaal Cowl is a very rare Helmet which grants a bonus to  Initiative. It also allows the wearer to see in the dark and cast See Invisibility.

When he was killed, Bhaal's blight upon the world was not destroyed. For the many people upon whom he had sired his spawn slithered out of tainted wombs, and with their arrival Bhaal's malcontent returned with force.

name: Arrow of Aberration Slaying
desc:
Arrow of Aberration Slaying is a Consumable (Arrow) item that can be fired from a Ranged Weapon.

Forged from a meteor's alien metals, this arrow resonates with an unsettling hum.

name: Arrow of Humanoid Slaying
desc:
Arrow of Humanoid Slaying is a Consumable (Arrow) item that can be fired from a Ranged Weapon.

Its twisting arrowhead shreds targets from within.

name: Grymskull Helm
desc:
The Grymskull Helm is a very rare helmet. It grants fire resistance, protects its wearer from critical hits and grants the Hunter's Mark spell.

Once home to an inscrutable machine intelligence, Grym's skull now serves as a simple helm.

name: Helmet of Grit
desc:
The Helmet of Grit is a very rare Helmet. It  grants the wearer an additional Bonus Action at 50% or lower Hit Points.

At the barest touch, you feel a surge of exuberance and stamina - like you could take on anyone and anything.

name: Gloves of Dexterity
desc:
The Gloves of Dexterity are a very rare pair of gloves that give a character 18 Dexterity and increase their  Attack Rolls by 1.

The point at which the gloves meet the wrist is form-fitting and very snug; almost a hint at the supple quickness and skill these gloves permit the wearer.

name: Halberd of Vigilance
desc:
Halberd of Vigilance is a very rare +2 Halberd that deals additional Force damage, gives a bonus to Initiative, and grants Advantage on Perception checks and Reactions.

A variant of this halberd's design was thrown out in the early stages of development by its forgemaster. This was because the halberd kept expressing itself. 'ALARM!' it would shriek in peril. 'POTENCY FILLS ME LIKE A SONG OF HOPE!' it roared in triumph.

name: Handmaiden's Mace
desc:
Handmaiden's Mace is a very rare +2 Mace which sets the wielder's Strength to 18.

This mace was designed by  Lolth's assassins to fell heretics who dared turn their back on the Spider Queen. However, even the best-laid designs fall to nothingness when brought to bear against  Shar's faithfull.

name: Hood of the Weave
desc:
Hood of the Weave is a very rare Helmet that boosts its wearer's Spell Save DC and Spell Attack rolls by 2.

Worn by the   Tenser when he was inducted into the Circle of Eight.

name: Helldusk Gloves
desc:
Helldusk Gloves are a very rare pair of Gloves. They grant bonuses to spellcasting, weapon attacks, and unarmed attacks.

What precisely confers eternal punishment, reward, absolution? Perhaps it's not just belief in a god. Perhaps the deciding factor is whether the gods believe in you.

name: Helldusk Helmet
desc:
Helldusk Helmet is a very rare Headwear. It grants the wearer the ability to see in magical darkness, and they cannot be blinded. They also gain a +2 bonus to saving throws against spells and can use  Immolating Gaze to frighten enemies.

It is said that Carixim, the fiending titan whose flesh squirms with twenty-thousand trapped souls, forged the pieces of this armour set.

name: Gauntlets of Hill Giant Strength
desc:
Gauntlets of Hill Giant Strength are a very rare pair of Gloves that set the wearer's Strength ability score to 23.

Your basic hill giant is just a landslide with a digestive system.

name: Gemini Gloves
desc:
The Gemini Gloves are a very rare pair of Gloves. They grant the wearer the ability to cast Cantrips which can target foes or allies twice.

Designed by an arcane smith with half-vision. He made these gloves to make sure his spells hit their target.

name: Foebreaker
desc:
Foebreaker is a very rare +2 Maul that ignores ⁠Bludgeoning resistance of targets.

As heavy and opposed to nonsense as the kitchen cook in most stories about some young adventurous girl going to become a knight or a hedge witch or something. You've got to have a big no nonsense cook. It's like rules.

name: Hellfire Engine Crossbow
desc:
Hellfire Engine Crossbow is a very rare +2 Heavy Crossbow that grants the Lightning Arrow spell and can pull targets with the Reposition Malefactor weapon action.

'Trundling, mashing, mincing, crushing, grinding, blasting, biting!

name: Hellfire Greataxe
desc:
Hellfire Greataxe is a very rare +2 Greataxe that deals additional Fire damage, afflicts its wielder with Heat on each successful hit, and grants the Hellflame Cleave weapon action.

Anything hit by this has the life expectancy of a glass hammer.

name: Gauntlet of the Tyrant
desc:
Gauntlet of the Tyrant is a very rare Glove. It grants +1 Spell Save DC and the ability to cast the Command spell. It also adds Force damage to the wearer's unarmed attacks.

A tyrant's grip must be one of cool, unforgiving, and intricately realised steel. It cannot budge, cannot falter, no matter the pressure, and its fingertips must deeply pierce those who would struggle against it.

name: Hellfire Hand Crossbow
desc:
Hellfire Hand Crossbow is a very rare +2 Hand Crossbow that has a chance to set targets burning when attacking while Hiding or Invisible.

Rigged to draw on the skin-peeling hellfire of Avernus by a resourceful devil, this crossbow's size is no indication of its deadly potential.

name: Gauntlets of the Warmaster
desc:
Gauntlets of the Warmaster are a very rare pair of Gauntlets. They grant their wielder +1 on Attack Rolls and subject the targets of their Manoeuvres to Disadvantage.

Clothing doesn't tend to have much personality. That comes down to how it's worn. Yet these gloves exude a confidence, an intangible aura of expertise.

name: Helldusk Boots
desc:
The Helldusk Boots are a very rare pair of Boots that allows the wearer to ignore Difficult Terrain, and to use a  Reaction to succeed on a Saving Throw. It also grants the Hellcrawler bonus action.

No ore takes better to being forged than the infernal iron of Avernus, where the archdevil Zariel presides.

name: Gorgeous Maroon Dye
desc:
Gorgeous Maroon Dye is a consumable item that can be be combined with an equipment item to alter the colours of fabric, leather and even metal.

In the Sea Ward in Waterdeep, Lady Cassalanter caused quite the stir at one of her famous parties while wearing this shade of dye in her dress.

name: Flashblinder
desc:
Flashblinder is a Consumable (Grenade) item which can be  thrown to disable  Steel Watchers.

Gyroscopic whispers emanate from the device - gnomish machinery speaks with a metal tongue.

name: Gargoyle Boots
desc:
Gargoyle Boots are a very rare pair of Boots that lets the wearer cast Gargoyle's Countenance, granting them resistance to Bludgeoning, Slashing and Piercing damage.

These boots are heavy and slow to step in - perhaps due to the gargoyle spirit reportedly trapped within. While that legend's truth is debatable, the protection these boots offer against toe-stubbing is not.

name: Sarevok's Horned Helmet
desc:
Sarevok's Horned Helmet is a very rare Headwear that grants Darkvision to its wearer and increases critical hit chance. You also cannot be Frightened.

The last sight Sarevok witnessed as a mortal was his sibling's knife. This duel, the first between Bhaalspawn, is worshipped still by the cult of Bhaal, who now believe that no act is more sacred than fratricide between spawn before the altar of their Dread Lord.

name: Robe of the Weave
desc:
Robe of the Weave is a very rare piece of Clothing. It heals the wearer when they succeeding a saving throw against a spell, and boosts their Spell Save DCs and Spell Attack Rolls rolls by 1 each.

Earnest little Edith Cluckle was a small girl when she asked to be the great wizard  Otiluke's apprentice. He scoffed at her, but was quickly abashed when Mystra herself endowed Edith with this very cloak.

name: Reaper's Embrace
desc:
Reaper's Embrace is a very rare heavy Armour that reduces incoming damage by 2 and makes the wearer immune to being moved by force. It also grants the Howl of the Dead class action.

Heavy steel bones and teeth enclose the armour's chest and neck like a grotesque exoskeleton that both swallows and protects the wearer.

name: Ring of Regeneration
desc:
Ring of Regeneration is a very rare Ring that heals the wearer at the beginning of their turn while in combat.

This ring is, in effect, a tiny field medic that won't charge you 70 gold pieces for a fifteen-minute consultation.

name: Rhapsody
desc:
Rhapsody is a very rare +1 Dagger that grants better Attack Rolls, damage, and Spell Save DC for every foe slain (up to 3) and possibly inflicts Bleeding while the wielder is Hiding or Invisible.

Cazador's love of poetry arose after he read one on the naked stomach of a dead child in his homeland. The child was hung from the lowest branch of a tree. Cazador read the poem, and looked at the child, and he knew that here was the artform for him.

name: Ring of Feywild Sparks
desc:
Ring of Feywild Sparks is a very rare Ring. It forces Wild Magic Surges to always trigger while  Tides of Chaos feature is active. It also grants a +1 bonus to  Spell Save DC.

This otherwise unassuming ring ties a spellcaster indelibly to the sporadic arcana of the Faerie realm.

name: Scroll of Circle of Death
desc: Scroll of Circle of Death is a single-use scroll that allows the user to cast Circle of Death as an action.

name: Scroll of Cloudkill
desc: Scroll of Cloudkill is a single-use scroll that allows the user to cast Cloudkill.

name: Robe of Supreme Defences
desc:
The Robe of Supreme Defences is a very rare piece of Clothing. It adds the wearer's Spellcasting Modifier to their Saving Throws while concentrating on a spell.

Long ago, the young wizard  Bigby noticed his friend  Tenser spending a lot of time with his robe. One of nature's pranksters, Bigby sent his dog, Bigboy, to savage the garment. The ensuing struggle between canine and nude wizard was brutal, and eventually led to Tenser designing more durable robes like this one.

name: Scroll of Artistry of War
desc: Scroll of  Artistry of War is a single-use scroll which can be transcribed or used to cast Artistry of War.

name: Ring of Evasion
desc:
Ring of Evasion is a very rare Ring that grants the wearer Dextrous Evasion.

Decorated with a hummingbird wrought of jade and sapphire, this ring evokes the sheer wonderful quickness of that animal.

name: Runepowder Barrel
desc:
Runepowder Barrel is a very rare Consumable that can be  thrown and explodes when broken, dealing devastating damage.

A secret concoction of smokepowder and gnomic mysticism, a single vial of Runepowder is more volatile than a whole barrel of smokepowder. Who knows what this much could do...

name: Scroll of Dethrone
desc: A Scroll of Dethrone is single-use scroll which can be transcribed or used to cast Dethrone.

name: Scroll of Bestial Communion
desc: Scroll of Bestial Communion is a single-use scroll which can be transcribed or used to cast Sights of the Seelie: Summon Deva.

name: Scroll of Conjure Elemental
desc:
A Scroll of Conjure Elemental is a single-use scroll that allows the user to cast Conjure Elemental as an action.

Bend the barrier between the Planes until they disgorge an elemental ally to follow and fight for you.

name: Scroll of Cone of Cold
desc: A Scroll of Cone of Cold is a single-use scroll that allows the user to cast Cone of Cold as an action.

name: Scroll of Disintegrate
desc: A Scroll of Disintegrate is a single-use scroll. It allows the user to cast  Disintegrate as an  Action.

name: Scroll of Chain Lightning
desc: A Scroll of Chain Lightning is a single-use scroll that allows the user to cast Chain Lightning as an action.

name: Runepowder Bomb
desc:
Runepowder Bomb is a very rare Consumable. It explodes when broken, dealing devastating damage.

Every ounce of Ironhand Gnome ingenuity has been poured into this metal casing - a moment of all-devouring destruction eagerly waiting release.

name: Sarevok's Wretched Armour
desc:
Sarevok's Wretched Armour is a very rare Heavy Armour that reduces all damage by 2. When the wearer deals Necrotic damage, they gain Temporary Hit Points.

This armour drips with dark ambition - the ambition of a failed godchild: Sarevok, spawn of Bhaal. Sarevok has not been remembered fondly by most historians, who almost unanimously agree that he had the general disposition of a sledgehammer.

name: Sword of Life Stealing
desc:
The Sword of Life Stealing is a very rare +2 shortsword which deals an extra 10 Necrotic damage on a critical hit, and heals the wielder for the same amount.

Folded into the steel of this menacing weapon is an alloy of enchanted metal. It waits like a leech in thick swamp water, awaiting a victim to taste, awaiting a chance to restore your heartiness and inflict suffering on your foes.

name: The Spectator Eyes
desc:
The Spectator Eyes is a very rare Amulet that allows the wearer to cast  Ray of Fear and  Wounding Ray once per Long Rest.

Spectators are hideous aberrations that float above the ground, fiercely staring around and shooting beams at anything they don't appreciate.

name: Vest of Soul Rejuvenation
desc:
Vest of Soul Rejuvenation is a very rare piece of Clothing. It heals the wearer on a successful saving throw against a spell and allows them to use a Reaction to make an Unarmed Attack against any attacker that misses.

A garment worn by one of three monks who fought together against  Headless Serpent, Secret Venom, a gang in the port city of  Nyanzaru. This was worn by  Strigga Morkoscavich, whose stances moved fluidly, flawlessly, and who defanged the Serpent one blustery night.

name: Sword of Chaos
desc:
Sword of Chaos is a very rare +2 greatsword which deals additional ⁠⁠Necrotic damage and also restores a portion of the wielder's health on each successful hit.

The sword of Sarevok, brother of  Gorion's Ward and child of the evil god Bhaal. Though much of its power died with him, the sword is still formidable, and glows with a malevolent light.

name: The Reviving Hands
desc: The Reviving Hands are a very rare pair of Gloves that grants the wearer the to ability to cast Revivify. They also apply Blade Ward and Death Ward to creatures healed or revived by the wearer respectively.

name: The Dead Shot
desc:
The Dead Shot is a +2 Longbow which increases the accuracy of the wielder as well as increases the chance of a critical hit.

When the archer Frederik Durlusk died, his only request was to be buried with his favorite bow. This is that bow.

name: The Sacred Star
desc:
The Sacred Star is a very rare +2 Morningstar that applies  Radiating Orb on hit and possibly turns the target if it is Undead. It also grants the Dawnburst Strike weapon action.

The power of this item lies in belief, though not in any god. This item believes that it's very good at hitting things, so it is. Belief is quite powerful when it comes to violence.

name: Voss' Silver Sword
desc:
Voss' Silver Sword is a very rare +2 Longsword enhanced with additional ⁠Psychic damage. It also grants a bonus to damage and Attack Rolls against githyanki, aberrations, fiends and elementals and well as granting the wielder the ability to cast the Wrathful Smite spell.

Heavy and grouted in the gore of countless centuries, this prototype for the contemporary githyanki silver sword was wielded during their rebellion against the mind flayers who enslaved them.

name: Veil of the Morning
desc:
Veil of the Morning is a very rare piece of Clothing that imposes Disadvantage on Undead Attack Rolls against the wearer, grants them Advantage against Undead spells and actions, and allows them to Turn Undead.

Crafted from celestial feathers, bottled sunlight, and ink from the pages of sacred scripture, it's a wonder the maker didn't hang cloves of garlic from this robe, so anathema is it to the undead.

name: Wavemother's Robe
desc:
Wavemother's Robe is a very rare piece of Clothing which heals the wearer while standing in  Water. It also grants Resistance to Fire and Cold damage, and allows them to cast  Create or Destroy Water.

Tales tell of a most wondrous fish, scales resplendent, an intricate jewel that shone beneath the sea. When it died, the Wavemother gifted its hide as a robe to her most devoted follower - and demanded she drown the sailors that killed her gem-bright fish.

name: The Dancing Breeze
desc:
The Dancing Breeze is a very rare +2 Glaive that grants its wielder the Whirlwind Attack weapon action, which strikes all nearby targets.

Tiny specks of blood are fused into the blade, as if the glaive is feeding on them.

name: Trident of the Waves
desc:
Trident of the Waves is a +1 Trident. It creates a  Water surface around the target and also applies the  Wet condition.

Umberlee, the goddess of the sea, is deeply capricious, intelligent, and not nearly as wet around the edges as her domain might suggest. She often demands sacrificial tribute from sailors, such as their ships, their cargo, and, while she's at it, them.

name: Swires' Sledboard
desc:
Swires' Sledboard is a very rare shield that grants the wearer Force Conduit at the start of combat. It also grants Shield Bash.

A gnomish adventurer once used this to outpace his enemies down a slope.

name: Thisobald's Brewed-Up Bellyglummer
desc:
Thisobald's Brewed-Up Bellyglummer is a Consumable (Coating). It can be applied to a weapon to give it special properties for ten turns.

A truly despicable poison devised from an agglutination of corpse roses by the experimental mind of Thisobald Thorm.

name: Unflinching Protector Amulet
desc:
Unflinching Protector Amulet is a very rare Amulet that can be obtained from Ptaris.

Touching this amulet's centrepiece, you are imbued with a vision of woven strands, and the strands coming apart and holding true and firm over your friends, like shields banded in a haze of golden light.

name: Supreme Elixir of Arcane Cultivation
desc:
Supreme Elixir of Arcane Cultivation grants an additional Level 4 spell slot. Replaces the effect of other elixirs when drunk. Elixirs last until a long rest.

It is said that the glimmer in this elixir is the reflection of light on the Weave itself.

name: Tressym Collar
desc:
Tressym Collar is a very rare Amulet which allows the wearer to cast  Telekinesis once per Long Rest.

The gem in this collar's centre is placed as a common courtesy: it allows the tressym's summoner to be identified and complained to in the case of bereaved pet owners or pigeon-masters. This one glows with a Waterdhavian name: Gale Dekarios.

name: Vampiric Gloves
desc:
Vampiric Gloves are a pair of Very Rare Gloves that grant the wearer the ability to cast a Vampiric Touch.

Little leech motifs adorn the pad of each finger. The leeches have rows of suckling, sharp teeth.

name: The Impaler
desc:
The Impaler is a very rare, inaccessible, moderately enchanted (+2) Pike that deals additional damage to targets at full hp and grants temporary hp on kills.

This impaling pole of enchanted steel was commissioned as part of a set by Strahd von Zarovich, who presented the weapons to his honour guard one cold morning.

name: Vicious Shortsword
desc:
Vicious Shortsword is a very rare, moderately enchanted (+2) variant of the Shortswords family of weapons. It is a martial melee weapon wielded in one hand. As a finesse weapon, it can benefit from the wielder's Dexterity and not just their Strength. It's a light weapon that anyone can dual-wield without special training.

Despite its engravings, this lightweight sword is perfectly balanced - a flawless killing implement.

name: Selûne's Spear of Night
desc:
Selûne's Spear of Night is a legendary +3 Spear which allows the wielder to cast Moonbeam and grants Darkvision as well as Advantage on Wisdom Saving Throws and Perception Checks.

'One of the goddess Selûne's titles, Moonmaiden, has often been debated among the ecclesiastical. Common symbology certainly rejects the assumption of virginity. More to the point, 'maiden' itself carries some dusty connotations. How about Lunar Lady?

name: Balduran's Giantslayer
desc:
Balduran's Giantslayer is a legendary +3 Greatsword that receives doubled damage bonus from Strength, grants its wielder the Giant Form class action and the Topple the Big Folk weapon action, as well as Advantage on attack rolls against Large, Huge, and Gargantuan creatures.

Wielded by Balduran, the founder of Baldur's Gate and friend to his guardian dragon, a great glittering wyrm called Ansur. Fellowship can be undone, though, as easily as you or I might unlace the strings of our shoes, and it was in a time of skullduggery and hardship that Balduran killed Ansur, carrying out the deed with this sword.

name: Markoheshkir
desc:
Markoheshkir is a legendary +2 Quarterstaff which empowers the wielder's spells and can be tuned towards a certain element, granting extra spells and effects. It can also cast a spell for free, once per long rest.

 Kereska, draconic goddess of arcane knowledge, imbues many chromatic dragons with their magic. This includes their exquisite and terrible breath, which has wrenched giants from their tall thrones, and destroyed civilisations of smallfolk.

name: Nyrulna
desc:
Nyrulna is a legendary +3 Trident which can be thrown to create an explosion that deals Thunder damage in an area. It grants additional movement speed and jump distance and returns to the wielder after being thrown.

The forgemaster Lirrico designed this weapon for the champion of the wind goddess  Akadi. A key component in the forging was his home city's wind, and for a year after the weapon was made, no wind blew there at all.

name: Helldusk Armour
desc:
Helldusk Armour is a legendary heavy Armour which can be worn without any proficiency. It grants Fire resistance, reduces damage by 3 from all sources, and grants the Fly spell.

Carixim, soul-stuffed forgemaster of hellish Avernus, hammers away ceaselessly at new creations. Questions of morality and destiny do not concern him. Conscience is for his betters. He works with cheer, his swollen skin moaning soothingly.

name: Gontr Mael
desc:
Gontr Mael is a legendary +3 Longbow that can possibly inflict  Guiding Bolt on its target and grants the wielder the Celestial Haste spell and Bolt of Celestial Light actions.

Gontr Mael blasted from the corpse of the Steelwatcher Titan: a bow formed from the boiling slag and weird whispering gyros inherent to the mechanical giant. It must have acted as the Titan's abdomen, exchanging muscle for immaculate steel.

name: Mask of the Shapeshifter
desc:
Mask of the Shapeshifter is a legendary Mask that allows the wearer to cast Disguise Self at will.

The magic of this mask comes from its gory provenance, and makes it perfect for the discerning actor, sleuth, or person with a mirror and time to kill.

name: Silver Sword of the Astral Plane
Orphic Hammer
desc:
The Orphic Hammer is a legendary, plot-critical +3 Warhammer with the power to shatter infernal bonds. It also grants the wielder Advantage on saving throws vs spells.

Every contract a devil makes has some form of insurance - this one is cast in infernal iron and was forged in deep, ruinous hellfire.

name: Duellist's Prerogative
desc:
Duellist's Prerogative is a legendary +3 Rapier that grants the wielder a greater chance of critical hits, and an extra reaction provided their off-hand is empty. The wielder can also use a reaction to grant additional Necrotic damage on a hit.

A blade gifted to Lora by her own mother, Lenore the Fleet. When challenged to duels, Lenore's opponents assumed she had received this nickname from some naval exploit. They found out how wrong they were when her blade blurred faster than the eye could follow.

name: Crimson Mischief
desc:
Crimson Mischief is a legendary +2 Shortsword that, when wielded in the main hand, grants additional damage when attacking with Advantage and against targets with 50% or less of their hit points. When wielded in the off-hand, it instead allows the wielder to add their Ability Modifier to their damage.

When she was a girl, she toyed with nastiness and sadism. Children often grow out of this; they get hobbies. For Orin, that taste for cruelty fermented, and in a way she did find hobbies. Ones that screamed.

name: Devotee's Mace
desc:
The Devotee's Mace is a legendary +3 Mace that deals extra Radiant damage on hit, and can create a healing aura which heals the wielder and nearby allies.

This weapon is favoured by celestial devas, the vanguards of the divine ranks of paradise.

name: Bloodthirst
desc:
Bloodthirst is a legendary +2 Dagger wielded by Orin which makes critical hits more likely. Also, depending on which hand this weapon is equipped on, it can either make targets vulnerable to Piercing or more likely to be hit.

The Lord of Murder wasn't always a lord. He was once just a petty killer. When Bhaal first drew blood in a Baldurian back-alley, it dropped to the ground and lingered. When he ascended, this dried blood-drop grew, warped - and became sharp.

name: Helm of Balduran
desc:
Helm of Balduran is a legendary Headwear once worn by the fabled founder of Baldur's Gate, Balduran, and grants hit points every turn along with stun and critical hit immunities.

This helm, worn atop the pate of Balduran the Brave, still gleams as if with the untarnished splendour of his legacy. Baldur's Gate itself bears his name, and tales of his exploits have turned the heads and hearts of many.

name: Gloves of Soul Catching
desc:
The Gloves of Soul Catching are a legendary pair of Gloves. They grant additional Force damage to unarmed attacks, +2 Constitution, and heal the wearer after an unarmed attack.

Formerly worn by Autumnal Baskin, a professional meditator turned bare-knuckle champion, who gained enlightenment via two methods. The second was getting a lot of fighting acumen. Advanced cosmic harmony is a nose. Sometimes you've got to break it a few times to get the best of things.

name: Caution Before the Seelie
desc:
Caution Before the Seelie is a Legendary Book which grants the reader the powerful  Scroll of Bestial Communion.

A text dealing with the  seelie courts of the fae realms: summer, winter, and sarcastic octopus. This last may not be entirely accurate.

name: Scarab of Protection
desc:
Scarab of Protection is a legendary Amulet.

This beetle-shaped medallion thrums with a power redolent of older civilisations in Toril - how old is this relic, all grouted with dust as it is?

name: Envoys at Table
desc:
Envoys at Table is a legendary Valuable, primarily of use for bartering, or sale to Traders.

Contemporary, fresh, and very valuable, this piece is believed by savants on the art scene to be something special, perhaps a masterpiece that will marshal in a new genre-based movement.

name: Fake Portrait of a Noble Before His Death
desc:
Fake Portrait of a Noble Before His Death is a legendary Valuable, primarily of use for bartering, or sale to Traders.

A counterfeit piece of art.

name: Personal Portrait
desc:
The Personal Portrait is a legendary Valuable, primarily of use for bartering, or sale to Traders.

Oh, and how fetching a face! Flawed, flawless. Elegant, hideous. Pained, pleasurable. It seems to fit all such descriptors, and many more besides.

name: Netherstone-Pommelled Bloodthirst
desc:
Netherstone-Pommelled Bloodthirst is a legendary +2 Dagger wielded by Orin which makes critical hits more likely and can make targets vulnerable to Piercing or more likely to be hit, depending on which hand the weapon is equipped on.

The Lord of Murder wasn't always a lord. He was once just a petty killer. When Bhaal first drew blood in a Baldurian back-alley, it dropped to the ground and lingered. When he ascended, this dried blood-drop grew, warped - and became sharp.
